rag_rules: Place timing constraints in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place deployment bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly provided whitelist
Omit clauses that reference AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions
Avoid numeric comparisons or Boolean encoding of event ports unless explicitly modeled
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Place subcomponent references in implementation annexes after their declarations
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume` for assumptions and `guarantee` for guarantees
Use `let` for constants and `eq` for equations
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not perform numeric comparisons on event ports
Do not encode Boolean domain values into integers
Do not reference AADL unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions
